title: Per-chunk recompute verification (2nd worker/backend hash match) wired into ledger acceptance
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The HARD RULE binds all computation-run and release work: no digit is accepted or published unless two independent computations are bit-identical, BBP/Bellard hex spot-checks pass, every chunk is recompute-verified and hashed, the published prefix matches a known reference, and the guard-digit analysis is recorded; an author may not verify their own run. This task enforces the per-chunk half of that rule: every chunk is recomputed by a second independent worker/backend and only a hash match admits it into the merge and ledger. It depends on verify-004 and merge-008.
objective: Wire per-chunk recompute verification (second independent worker/backend hash match) into ledger acceptance so unverified chunks never enter the merge.
acceptanceCriteria: Each chunk is recomputed by a second independent worker/backend; acceptance into the merge requires a hash match; a mismatch rejects the chunk and never enters the ledger as verified.
Every accepted chunk has a VerificationRecord linked in the hash-chained ledger.
output: A PR adding per-chunk recompute verification wired into hash-chained ledger acceptance.
